attacking_style: Aggressive from extreme distance, notably attacking from kilometer zero on HC mountain stages. Favors short punchy climbs 15-25km from finish and early breakaways rather than late decisive moves.
team_tactics: Limited team support for finales; relies on self-sufficiency in breakaways. EF lacks organized firepower to hold off WorldTour lead-out trains. Often positioned as opportunistic raider rather than protected GC asset.
terrain_preference: Short punchy climbs and HC mountain stages suit best. Cobbled classics showed surprising versatility (nearly took yellow). Struggles on repeated climbs later in stages when fresher climbers dominate.
vulnerability: Exposed on second ascents of same climb when team hierarchy/strength differences emerge. Lacks finishing speed against organized teams. Struggles to convert breakaway leads without team support in final kilometers.
key_patterns: Attacks from extreme distance: kilometer zero on HC stages, 20-25km out on punchy terrain.
KOM jersey tactics: strategic climb selection, sometimes skipping finish points for bigger opportunities.
Breakaway specialist prioritizing time recovery over stage wins when needed (2020 TDF twice).
Strong form resilience: attacks day after mechanicals, contests finishes while managing GC goals.
Fades in structured finales against organized teams, losing leads in final 15 seconds repeatedly.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 TDF showed tactical maturity in KOM competition but ultimately lost on tiebreaker, suggesting ceiling as classification hunter rather than stage winner evolution
